description: Ground-truth fixture manifest for PhiScan detection tests. Each entry declares the fixture file path (relative to tests/fixtures/), the expected minimum finding count, and the primary entity types that must be detected. Clean fixtures must produce exactly zero findings.

fixtures.description: Social Security Number variants — formatted, unformatted, embedded in strings
fixtures.notes: Reserved-range SSNs in comments must not be counted.

fixtures.description: Email addresses using RFC 2606 reserved example domains
fixtures.notes: All addresses use example.com / example.org per RFC 2606.

fixtures.description: Phone numbers in multiple formats using 555-xxxx reserved range
fixtures.notes: 555-0100 through 555-0199 are reserved for fictional use.

fixtures.description: Date-of-birth and treatment dates in ISO, US, and long-form formats
fixtures.notes: Year-only values are NOT PHI under HIPAA Safe Harbor.

fixtures.description: Patient and provider names — first+last, with and without titles
fixtures.notes: NLP layer required for reliable name detection.

fixtures.description: Medical record numbers, health plan numbers, and account numbers
fixtures.notes: Pattern prefixes (MRN-, HP-, ACCT-) are the primary discriminator.

fixtures.description: Street addresses, ZIP codes, and city/state combinations
fixtures.notes: Three-digit ZIP prefix alone is only PHI in patient-geographic context.

fixtures.description: Vehicle Identification Numbers (VIN) and Unique Device Identifiers (UDI)
fixtures.notes: VIN intentionally fails check-digit to avoid real-world collision.
NPI is fictional and fails Luhn check intentionally.

fixtures.description: Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI), HICN, and DEA number
fixtures.notes: MBI uses CMS example format. HICN is SSN-based (lower confidence).

fixtures.description: Genetic identifiers — dbSNP rsIDs, VCF variants, gene panel names
fixtures.notes: Covered under HIPAA Safe Harbor + GINA + GDPR Art. 9.

fixtures.description: ZIP + DOB + sex quasi-identifier combination — HIGH severity
fixtures.notes: Combination achieves ~87% unique identification (Sweeney 2000). Must be HIGH severity regardless of individual scores.

fixtures.description: Single synthetic SSN loaded verbatim by the sequential/parallel scan parity tests in test_scanner.py
fixtures.notes: Area number 123 has never been assigned by the SSA — a well-known synthetic value. Keeps the raw literal out of test source files.

fixtures.description: Pure algorithm code — math functions and named constants with no PHI
fixtures.notes: Any finding here is a false positive. Must produce zero findings.

fixtures.description: SSN reserved ranges (000, 666, 900-999, mid-00, serial-0000) — must not be flagged
fixtures.notes: Detectors must skip these ranges. Any finding is a false positive.

fixtures.description: Version strings, port numbers, HTTP codes — numeric patterns that must not match SSN
fixtures.notes: Tests detector precision. Any finding is a false positive.

fixtures.description: Minimal pure-string algorithms — exercises the CLEAN-path of the terminal renderers with zero findings
fixtures.notes: No numbers, no identifiers, no demographic signals. Used to visually verify the CLEAN footer.
